outbound_official.notes: Source is the official MRE Paraguay 'Dirección de Tratados' bilateral visa-suppression agreements document (Acuerdos Bilaterales Suscritos por el Paraguay en Materia de Supresión de Visas), updated September 2019, published on the official Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores portal. The document lists bilateral agreements - each is reciprocal, so countries where ordinary (C=Común) or all (T=Todos) passport types are covered apply to outbound Paraguayan ordinary passport holders. Argentina and Uruguay are MERCOSUR members ((*) footnote: travel with national ID card only); Cuba, Taiwan, Philippines, Vietnam, Indonesia, Jordan, Lebanon, Egypt, Morocco, India, Georgia cover only diplomatic/official passports for Paraguay (not ordinary). Venezuela agreement suspended 10/Jan/2019. Montenegro, Azerbaijan, Serbia, Qatar agreements listed as PENDIENTE (not yet in force) as of September 2019. A more recent updated PDF (2026) exists at migraciones.gov.py; the 2026 version adds UAE, Georgia (DOS), Mongolia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Trinidad and Tobago, Bahamas, Malaysia, Philippines (ordinary, via 2025 decrees) and confirms Venezuela suspension.

e_visa.notes: Foreign nationals whose nationality requires a visa may submit an online pre-application through the MRE online portal at mre.gov.py/visasenlinea. Physical completion at a Paraguayan embassy or consulate is still required. This is NOT a fully electronic e-visa - it is a pre-application portal only.

visa_policy.notes: Primary authoritative source is the official Paraguayan immigration authority PDF (migraciones.gov.py, updated January 2026) which lists all bilateral visa suppression agreements by law/decree number and date. Paraguay grants visa-free access to 69 nationalities for tourism. Under Law 7314/2024, USA, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand are granted a temporary visa-free tourism measure valid until August 13, 2027 (for tourism only; other purposes require a visa). MERCOSUR/associate members (Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Uruguay) have no stay limit and may use national identity cards. Key changes as of January 2026: Venezuela now requires a visa (effective Jan 10, 2026); Philippines and Malaysia gained ordinary passport visa-free access (30 days each) per Decretos 5219 and 5220 of December 29, 2025; Georgia and Mongolia gained visa-free access via earlier laws; Trinidad and Tobago gained visa-free access per Ley 7439/December 2024; Saint Kitts and Nevis gained visa-free access per Ley 7205/November 2023. Russia is confirmed visa-free (not visa-on-arrival) per Ley 5227/2014. Qatar, Thailand, Vietnam, Indonesia, and Oman do NOT have visa-on-arrival confirmed by the January 2026 official PDF - ordinary passport holders of these nationalities require a regular visa per the authoritative source, though some MRE consulate sub-pages list Qatar and Oman as VOA at Silvio Pettirossi airport (discrepancy noted in gaps).

cbi.notes: Paraguay does not offer citizenship-by-investment. Citizenship is obtained through naturalization after holding legal residence for at least 3 years (per Paraguayan Constitution Article 148 and Migration Law 6984/2022). The Paraguay Investor Pass grants permanent residency, not citizenship directly.

programs.name: Paraguay Investor Pass
programs.type: real_estate / financial instruments / tourism / productive investment
programs.notes: Launched April 17, 2026 via MIC Resolution No. 0283/2026. Four investment modalities: (1) Productive investments (industry/commerce/services) from USD 70,000 with obligation to generate at least 5 formal jobs; (2) Financial instruments (stock market) from USD 200,000, no employment requirement; (3) Real estate from USD 200,000 (for economic activity, not personal/family use); (4) Tourism sector projects from USD 150,000. Grants permanent residency directly without prior temporary residency. Process mostly electronic; physical presence required only for national ID card issuance. Dividend tax rate reduced from 15% to 8% for residents. Path to citizenship via naturalization after 3 years of permanent residency. Official page ECONNREFUSED on June 2026 re-verification - program details preserved from prior verified fetch; not changed.

programs.name: Standard Permanent Residency
programs.notes: Permanent residency available by demonstrating economic solvency: minimum USD 5,000 in bank/cooperative account, OR university-level degree with employment contract, OR property ownership (minimum 10 hectares), OR company incorporation with minimum USD 5,000 capital. After 3 years of permanent residency, naturalization citizenship is possible per Constitution Article 148.

programs.name: SUACE Investor Permanent Residency
programs.notes: Special permanent residence process under Migration Law No. 6984/2022 for foreign investors using the Unified System for Business Opening and Closure (SUACE). Grants direct permanent residency without prior temporary residency requirement. Minimum investment amount not specified in available official content.

The mre.gov.py/visas-new/ page lists Qatar under diplomatic-only exemptions; the January 2026 PDF reportedly shows REQUIERE VISA for Oman. An updated official confirmation from migraciones.gov.py would resolve this.

mre.gov.py/visas-new/ page erroneously lists Venezuela as visa-free (as of June 2026) and Philippines under diplomatic-only - both are outdated entries. The MRE announcement page and the migraciones PDF are the authoritative overrides.
Processing time for Paraguay Investor Pass not officially published.
Dual citizenship policy in Paraguay not confirmed from an official government source.
No official fast-track / digital nomad / skilled worker visa program found.
SUACE minimum investment amount not found in official source.
The online visa application (mre.gov.py/visasenlinea) requires physical completion at an embassy - it is NOT a full e-visa system.

No credential-based conditional entry rules found on any official Paraguay government source (no rule granting entry to holders of US/Schengen/UK visas, Green Cards, or similar third-country credentials).
No OCI or special diaspora/heritage status conditional entry rule found on any official Paraguay government source.

The specific passport types covered by diplomatic exemptions for Saudi Arabia, Kazakhstan, Morocco, Vietnam, Qatar, India, Indonesia, Jordan, Lebanon, Egypt are listed on mre.gov.py/visas-new/ as 'diplomatic, official and service' but the migraciones PDF does not always break down the specific passport types for these - the MRE page is trusted for the diplomatic/service breakdown.
UN Laissez-Passer holders are exempt from visa requirements (Decreto N°3870/13/08/2015) - not classified in standard visa_policy fields as this is not a nationality-based rule.

Thailand visa status for ordinary passports unclear: Wikipedia cross-check lists Thailand as VOA (15 days); no official MRE or migraciones source confirmed this. Classified as visa-required per safety rule pending official confirmation.
Paraguay Investor Pass official page (migraciones.gov.py/paraguay-investor-pass) returned ECONNREFUSED on June 2026 re-verification - program details preserved from prior verified fetch, not updated.

visa_types.name: Visa de No Residente – Turista
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals whose nationality requires a visa to enter Paraguay for tourism purposes. Allows stays of up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Application submitted via MRE online pre-application portal (mre.gov.py/visasenlinea), then completed in person at a Paraguayan embassy or consulate. Processing recommended at least 30 days before travel. Applicant must be a resident or legal visitor in the country of the consulate applied to. Not a fully electronic e-visa.

visa_types.name: Visa de No Residente – Técnico
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Paraguay for technical, business, or professional activities requiring a visa.
visa_types.notes: Listed alongside the tourist category as 'Turista o Técnico' (Tourist or Technical) in the MRE online visa portal. Same application process via the online portal followed by consular appearance.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporaria (Temporary Residency)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who intend to reside and work in Paraguay temporarily, under an employment contract or similar arrangement.
visa_types.notes: Requires employment promise with salary details, employer credentials, and technical/educational certification. After obtaining temporary residency, holders may progress to permanent residency. Processed through Paraguayan consulates or immigration authority (migraciones.gov.py).

visa_types.name: Residencia Permanente – Solvencia Económica
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency for foreign nationals who can demonstrate economic solvency, suitable for retirees or those with passive income.
visa_types.notes: Requires minimum USD 5,000 in a Paraguayan bank or cooperative account as proof of economic solvency. Alternatively accepted: university degree with employment contract, property ownership (minimum 10 hectares), or company incorporation with USD 5,000 capital. Permanent residency granted directly; no separate rentista/retiree category is defined - economic solvency serves this function. Path to citizenship after 3 years of permanent residency.

visa_types.name: Paraguay Investor Pass
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency for foreign investors who make qualifying investments in Paraguay across multiple sectors.
visa_types.notes: Launched April 17, 2026 via MIC Resolution No. 0283/2026. Four investment modalities: (1) Productive investments from USD 70,000 with at least 5 formal jobs created; (2) Financial instruments (stock market) from USD 200,000; (3) Real estate from USD 200,000 for economic activity; (4) Tourism sector from USD 150,000. Grants permanent residency directly without prior temporary residency. Process is mostly electronic; physical presence required only for national ID card issuance. Dividend tax rate reduced to 8% for residents. Path to citizenship via naturalization after 3 years.

visa_types.name: Residencia Permanente para Inversionistas – SUACE
visa_types.purpose: Special permanent residency for foreign investors using Paraguay's Unified System for Business Opening and Closure (SUACE).
visa_types.notes: Available under Migration Law No. 6984/2022. Grants direct permanent residency without requiring prior temporary residency. Minimum investment amount not published in official sources. Processed through the SUACE business registration platform.

visa_types.name: Residencia por Vínculo Familiar
visa_types.purpose: Residency for spouses, children, or dependents of Paraguayan nationals or foreign residents.
visa_types.notes: Available for spouses of Paraguayan nationals, children of Paraguayan nationals, and minors (with parental authorization). Specific documentary requirements apply per relationship type. Processed through Paraguayan consulates or migraciones.gov.py.

visa_types.name: Residencia Precaria
visa_types.purpose: A temporary provisional residency status for foreign nationals while their main residency application is being processed.
visa_types.notes: Renewable 6-month periods. Requires origin documents and criminal background clearance. Acts as an interim status while permanent or temporary residency is adjudicated.

visa_types.name: Prórroga de Turista (Tourist Extension)
visa_types.purpose: Extension of tourist status for visitors who have already entered Paraguay and need additional time beyond their initial authorized stay.
visa_types.notes: Available through the Department of Investigations' Foreign Registry Office. Duration of extension and fees not specified in available official sources.

visa_types.name: Visa Diplomática / Oficial
visa_types.purpose: For holders of diplomatic and official passports traveling to Paraguay on official government business.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct category in the MRE online visa portal ('Diplomática, Oficial'). Many nationalities that require a visa for ordinary passport holders have visa-free access for diplomatic/official/service passports under bilateral agreements (e.g., Cuba, Saudi Arabia, Azerbaijan, India, Indonesia, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Lebanon, Qatar, Vietnam, Egypt, Morocco, Serbia).

visa_types.name: Visa de Llegada Excepcional (Arrival Visa)
visa_types.purpose: An exceptional visa-on-arrival issued at Silvio Pettirossi International Airport in Asunción for specific nationalities under special circumstances.
visa_types.notes: Listed as 'excepcional' (exceptional) in the MRE online visa portal under arrival visas. Available exclusively at Asunción's Silvio Pettirossi International Airport. Only applicable to certain nationalities; the January 2026 official migraciones.gov.py document does not confirm specific countries for visa-on-arrival - previously cited countries (Qatar, Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia) are not confirmed by the 2026 official PDF. MRE consulate sub-pages list Qatar and Oman as VOA - this discrepancy is tracked in data_quality.gaps.
